mission.title: Install and Configure Cubefs on Kubernetes
mission.description: CubeFS is an open-source cloud-native distributed file and object storage system that provides scalable and reliable storage solutions. Installing CubeFS allows you to leverage its capabilities for managing large volumes of data in a cloud-native environment.

steps.title: Add the CubeFS Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the CubeFS Helm repository to your local Helm setup to access the CubeFS charts.
```bash
helm repo add cubefs https://cubefs.github.io/cubefs-helm
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install CubeFS using Helm
steps.description:
Install CubeFS in the 'cubefs' namespace, creating the namespace if it doesn't exist.
```bash
helm install cubefs cubefs/cubefs --namespace cubefs --create-namespace --version 3.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the CubeFS pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cubefs
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the CubeFS service to access it directly.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/cubefs --namespace cubefs 17010:17010
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the CubeFS release from your cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall cubefs --namespace cubefs
```

uninstall.title: Clean up resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining resources associated with CubeFS, including CRDs and the namespace.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep cubefs | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete namespace cubefs
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration and data.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace cubefs -o yaml > cubefs-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure you have the latest version of the CubeFS Helm chart.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade CubeFS
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the CubeFS installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade cubefs cubefs/cubefs --namespace cubefs --version 3.2.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the CubeFS pods to ensure they are running the new version.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace cubefs
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in Pending, check for insufficient resources or scheduling issues.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> --namespace cubefs
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the CubeFS service, ensure that the service is running and the port forwarding is set up correctly.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace cubefs
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the pod logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace cubefs
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of CubeFS will have all pods running in the 'cubefs' namespace, and you will be able to access the service through the forwarded port. Resource limits should be set appropriately to ensure optimal performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add cubefs https://cubefs.github.io/cubefs-helm
helm repo update

kubectl edit deployment cubefs --namespace cubefs
# Add resource limits under spec.containers:
# resources:
#   limits:
#     cpu: "500m"
#     memory: "512Mi"

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm CLI installed to deploy CubeFS.
